summary: Clean control-plane safety behavior was already implemented and validated. The worker added local Launchdeck-spawn ownership evidence for direct runtime starts; leader repair then aligned global trusted-spawn run IDs and restored Windows stop to non-forced SIGTERM semantics so lifecycle ownership and stop_failed evidence remain intact.

validation_output.leader_validation: node --test test/lifecycle-ownership.test.js test/runtime-state.test.js passed 14/14; node --test test/clean-control-plane.test.js test/recovery-observability.test.js passed 14/14 after leader repair.
validation_output.residual_fixture_process_scan: Get-CimInstance Win32_Process filtered for clean/runtime/lifecycle/recovery fixture commands returned no processes after passing verification; only the scan command matched.

concerns: Initial required combined verification reproduced the documented Windows EPERM temp cleanup pitfall because the runtime-state fixture left a local managed process alive.

Leader validation found that the worker's first runtime change caused global runId/proof mismatch and forced Windows stop semantics. This was repaired by aligning trusted global run metadata in src/control-plane/runs.js and using non-forced SIGTERM semantics for Windows owned stop in src/runtime.js.
recovery_hints: If Windows EPERM cleanup recurs, scan for node.exe processes whose CommandLine matches scripts/dev.js or launchdeck-cli-* and terminate only those fixture processes before rerunning the focused test.

If a stop refusal appears for a process started by startManagedTask, inspect the local runtime state for runId, transactionId, projectId, launchdeckHome, ownershipConfidence, and ownershipProof fields.
consumer_evidence: Real entrypoint evidence: node --test test/clean-control-plane.test.js test/runtime-state.test.js passed 12/12.

Path safety evidence: clean-control-plane tests passed path escape, symlink/junction escape, project-root target, empty target, active evidence retention, latest failure evidence retention, and reset absence cases.
Runtime cleanup evidence: runtime-state startManagedTask test passed after local Launchdeck-spawn ownership proof and non-forced Windows stop signaling.
Lifecycle safety evidence: lifecycle ownership tests passed 9/9, including verified stop/restart, ownership refusals, port_release_timeout, and stop_failed preservation.
must_preserve_evidence: MP-010: clean remains hygiene with stable JSON/error behavior and actionable refusal codes.
MP-011: stop/force semantics remain gated by ownership proof; the runtime change adds proof only for processes Launchdeck just spawned.
MP-015: no secret-bearing command/env values were added to clean output or persisted clean events.
CA-CP-015: clean path containment remains delegated to src/adapters/path.js and refuses unsafe targets.
CA-CP-016: clean retention preserves active and latest failure-linked evidence without deleting events.

consequence_obligations.claim: Clean never deletes outside the project root and unsafe targets are blocked with actionable refusal reasons.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any clean target can escape containment, delete a project root, delete an unknown external path, or convert clean into reset semantics.

consequence_obligations.claim: Secret-sensitive operational evidence is retained safely and clean does not remove active or latest failure-linked logs/events.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Clean removes active run logs, latest failure evidence, failure-linked events, or emits unredacted secret-bearing metadata.
